subtopics.title: Hadoop
subtopics.explanation: Hadoop is an open-source framework for distributed storage (HDFS) and processing (MapReduce) of massive datasets across clusters of computers. It enables scaling from single servers to thousands of machines.
subtopics.example: Processing terabytes of log files across a 100-node cluster to identify user behavior patterns.
subtopics.code:
# MapReduce example (pseudocode)
# Map: Extract word from log
# Reduce: Count occurrences
map(key, line): emit(word, 1)
reduce(word, counts): emit(word, sum(counts))

subtopics.title: Spark
subtopics.explanation: Apache Spark is a unified analytics engine for large-scale data processing. It performs in-memory computing, making it much faster than Hadoop MapReduce for iterative algorithms and interactive queries.
subtopics.example: Process streaming data in real-time or run complex machine learning algorithms on terabyte-scale datasets.
subtopics.code:
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("example").getOrCreate()
df = spark.read.csv("huge_dataset.csv")
result = df.groupBy("category").count()
